mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate decline exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE falls below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index drop below 98.0 signals severe supply-side contraction that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances when INDPRO falls below the 98.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stagnant employment growth forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences or Rank IC remains negative over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, creating a persistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment exceeding 137000 thousand persons indicates full employment constraints that accelerate wage inflation and prompt rate hike fears, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances when PAYEMS crosses the 137000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and housing starts, where supply contracts while housing demand expands, creates sector rotation volatility that suppresses broad market returns over one week.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.
